outcome.notes: Operator selector cannot be changed via keyboard: the default '+' stays selected and Up/Down arrows do not change it. Step 4 was reported as pass by the user: with the default '+' operator, 3 + (-2) = 1 calculated correctly (note the test's expected 'Result: 5' assumes the '-' operator from step 2, which could not be selected, so the displayed result was 1). Step 5 clearing the number fields reset the result as expected. Follow-up (pattern control-not-responsive narrowing): the user confirmed it is NOT possible to select ANY operator via keyboard — the selector is stuck on the default '+' for every option, not just '-'. This makes the failure page/control-wide rather than option/value-specific, suggesting the keyboard handler is missing entirely on the operator control rather than broken for a single value.

steps.description: Cannot change the operator via keyboard. The default '+' stays selected and Up/Down arrows don't change it. Follow-up narrowing: NO operator option is selectable via keyboard (not just '-'); the control is stuck on the default '+' for all options, indicating the keyboard handler appears to be missing entirely on the operator control rather than broken for one value.
